https://www.citizensinformation.ie/en/ho.markdown:
# Ending a tenancy

A landlord must give a valid, written notice of termination to end a tenancy. The amount of notice a landlord must give depends on how long the tenancy has lasted.

If the tenancy has lasted less than 6 months, the landlord must give 28 days' notice. If the tenancy has lasted 6 months or more but less than 1 year, the landlord must give 35 days' notice. If the tenancy has lasted 1 year or more but less than 2 years, the landlord must give 42 days' notice. If the tenancy has lasted 2 years or more but less than 3 years, the landlord must give 56 days' notice. A landlord must give a notice period of 90 days where the tenancy has lasted 3 years or more.

A notice of termination must be in writing, signed by the landlord or their authorised agent, and state the date the tenancy will end.

A tenant who receives an invalid notice of termination can dispute it by referring the matter to the Residential Tenancies Board (RTB).
